- Moien, ech heesche Markus.
- An dir?
- Wéi heescht dir?
- Ech sinn d'Isabel.
- Komms du zu Neies?
- Vu wou kënnt du, Markus?
- Ech kommen aus Däitschland.
- An du?
- Kënns du aus Italien?
- Nee, ech kommen net aus Italien.
- Ech kommen aus Portugal.
- Ah, du bass Portugiesin.
- Da verstees du jo och Spuenesch, gell?
- Jo, e bësse, mir schwätze kee Spuenesch.
- Ech schwätze Portugisesch a Franséisch.
- An du?
- Wat fir Sproochen schwätz du?
- Ech schwätzen Däitsch an Englesch.
- Schwätz du kee Franséisch?
- Nee.
- An der Loh léiere mir all zwou nach Lëtzebuergesch.
- Richtig.
- Hello, my name is Markus.
- And you?
- What is your name?
- I am Isabel.
- Are you coming to Neies?
- Where are you from, Markus?
- I come from Germany.
- And you?
- Can you come from Italy?
- No, I do not come from Italy.
- I come from Portugal.
- Ah, you are Portuguese.
- Then you understand Spanish as well, right?
- Yes, a little, we do not speak Spanish.
- I speak Portuguese and French.
- And you?
- What languages do you speak?
- I speak German and English.
- Do you not speak French?
- No.
- In the school we all learn two more Luxembourgish.
- Correct.
